name: apply_patch
description: Applies a code patch to a specified file using a standard diff format (e.g., unified diff format). This tool is used for making targeted code modifications, applying bug fixes, or refactoring existing code segments within a file. Ensure the patch content is correctly formatted and is relative to the current content of the target file.

project_path.description: The root directory path of the project.

file_path.description: The relative path from project_path to the file that needs to be patched. Example: 'src/components/Button.js'.

patch_content.description: The content of the patch in a standard diff format (e.g., output of 'git diff'). The patch should clearly define the lines to be added, removed, or modified.

dry_run.description: If true, the tool will simulate applying the patch and report success or failure without making actual changes to the file. Useful for validating a patch.

hints.description: This tool is destructive as it modifies file content. It is not inherently idempotent; applying the same patch twice might lead to errors or unintended results if the file content has changed or if the patch is not designed for re-application.
